terms.definition: A function X: Ω → ℝ (or a countable set) assigning a numeric value to each outcome of a random experiment.
For discrete X, p(x) = P(X = x); non-negative values that sum to 1 over all possible x.
For continuous X, a non-negative function f where P(a ≤ X ≤ b) = ∫_a^b f(x) dx and ∫ f(x) dx = 1.
F(x) = P(X ≤ x); non-decreasing, right-continuous, with limits 0 as x → −∞ and 1 as x → ∞.
The set of values x for which p(x) > 0 (discrete) or f(x) > 0 (continuous)—where the random variable can actually occur.
